codeReview.summary: The `werk context <id>` command is well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage. The implementation correctly outputs rich JSON structural context for agents including tension details, ancestors (root-first), siblings (excluding self), children, all 10 computed dynamics, and mutation history. Code follows established patterns, uses sd-core APIs appropriately, and handles edge cases properly.

issues.description: The assimilation_depth field uses conditional logic to return None when depth is None AND total_mutations is 0. This matches the expected behavior but could be simplified by just checking depth == AssimilationDepth::None without the mutation count check, since depth is already computed from the mutations.

Forest::ancestors, siblings, and children use unwrap_or_default() which silently converts errors to empty vectors. While this is acceptable behavior for context display (showing empty lists on error), it might mask Forest API issues. Consider logging or propagating the error for better debugging.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed the established pattern of using Forest API for hierarchical operations (ancestors, siblings, children) and individual sd-core dynamics functions for all 10 dynamics computations. This aligns with AGENTS.md guidelines to never reimplement dynamics/store logic.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: werk-cli/src/main.rs:2170-2269 - Uses Forest::ancestors(), Forest::siblings(), Forest::children() and individual dynamics functions (compute_structural_tension, detect_structural_conflict, etc.)

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the rust-cli-worker skill procedure correctly: wrote 15 comprehensive integration tests first, then implemented the command, all tests passed, linting passed, manual verification completed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, handoff.verification.commandsRun shows all validators passed

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation demonstrates correct understanding of sd-core dynamics APIs including proper threshold configuration and result mapping to JSON. No documentation gaps discovered.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: werk-cli/src/main.rs:2188-2269 - Properly configures all thresholds and maps sd-core enum results to JSON strings

summary: Feature 'agent-context' successfully implements the `werk context <id>` command with complete JSON structural context output. All 6 validation contract assertions (VAL-AGENT-001 through VAL-AGENT-006) are covered by 15 integration tests. The implementation correctly derives ancestors (root-first), siblings (excluding self), children, and all 10 dynamics using sd-core APIs. Code is clean, well-structured, and passes all validators (test, clippy, fmt). No blocking issues found.
